page.title: Marbella Stag Weekend Yacht Charter: Budget Breakdown for Groups

data.meta_description: Plan a Marbella stag weekend yacht charter with our budget breakdown. Prices from €749/2h for groups up to 12. Book your boat today.
data.summary: A practical budget breakdown for a Marbella stag weekend yacht charter, covering boat options from €749/2h, inclusions, and tips for groups up to 12.
key_facts: Boat prices start at €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Maximum group size is 12 passengers on most boats.
Mangusta 80 (24m) costs €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a jet ski.
Departures are from Puerto Banús, Marbella's luxury marina.
Popular stag activities include swimming, sunbathing, and jet skiing.
Book via WhatsApp for boats not in Tier A or B.
Best season is May to October with 28-32°C sea temps.

faq.q: What is the cheapest boat for a stag weekend in Marbella?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, both at €749 for 2 hours, are the most affordable options. They accommodate up to 11 guests and include a skipper, drinks, and snacks, making them ideal for smaller stag groups.

faq.q: How many people can join a stag yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Most boats in our fleet, like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge, take up to 12 passengers. Smaller boats like the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax) suit intimate groups. Always check capacity when booking.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for a stag weekend?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). No hidden costs – just show up and enjoy.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks on the boat?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already provides a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise for your stag group. Just avoid glass bottles for safety.

faq.q: How long is a typical stag yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Most stag groups book for 4 to 8 hours. A 4-hour trip (e.g., €4,719 for Mangusta 80) covers swimming stops and cruising along the coast. For a full day, 8-hour charters from €2,299 (Tier A) offer more time for partying.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for a stag weekend yacht charter?
faq.a: May through October offers the best weather, with sea temperatures between 28°C and 32°C. July and August are peak season, so book early. April and November can still be pleasant but less predictable.

faq.q: Are there any extra costs I should budget for?
faq.a: No – the listed price includes everything except optional extras like a jet ski (free with Mangusta 80) or catering upgrades. Gratuities for the skipper are appreciated but not mandatory. Transfers to Puerto Banús are separate.

faq.q: How do I book a boat for a stag weekend in Marbella?
faq.a: For Tier A and B boats, you can book directly via our website. For other boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, contact us on WhatsApp for pricing and availability. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance.

data.body_html:
Planning a stag weekend in Marbella and want to hit the water without blowing your budget? A yacht charter is the ultimate group activity, combining sun, sea, and celebration along the Costa del Sol. This breakdown covers costs, boat options, and what's included, so your crew can focus on the fun. Whether you're after a quick 2-hour spin or a full-day party, our fleet in Puerto Banús has you covered.

Why Choose a Yacht Charter for Your Marbella Stag Weekend?

Marbella's coastline is made for group adventures. With 320 days of sunshine a year, you can cruise past landmarks like La Concha mountain, stop for a swim at Cala del Faro, or anchor near the Golden Mile. A private charter gives your group exclusive access – no crowded beach bars, just your own floating party. Plus, with a skipper handling the navigation, you can relax and enjoy the drinks and snacks included in every booking.

Budget Breakdown: What Does a Stag Yacht Charter Cost?

Prices vary by boat size and duration. Here's a clear breakdown for groups:

  • Tier A (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39): €749 for 2 hours, up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Perfect for groups of 9-11. Includes skipper, drinks, and snacks.
  • Tier B (Mangusta 80, any colour): €4,719 minimum 4 hours, with a free jet ski. Handles up to 12 guests on a 24m luxury yacht.
  • Other boats: Pricing on request via WhatsApp – ideal for larger groups wanting a Ferretti 94 or Canados 86.

All prices include Spanish VAT (21%), so no surprises. For a stag group of 10, splitting an 8-hour Tier A charter works out at just €230 per person – cheaper than a night out in Puerto Banús.

What's Included in Your Stag Weekend Charter?

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. You don't need to bring anything except your swimwear and a playlist. The Mangusta 80 even throws in a jet ski for free – perfect for stag antics on the water.

Best Boats for a Stag Weekend in Marbella

Our fleet offers options for every group size and budget:

BoatLengthMax PaxStarting Price
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 80 (White/Grey)24m12€4,719/4h
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Ferretti 9429m12On request

For a stag group, the Mangusta 80 is a top pick – plenty of deck space, a jet ski, and that wow factor. Smaller groups might prefer the Azimut 39 for its value.

How to Split Costs Among Your Stag Group

Dividing the bill is simple. For a 4-hour charter on the Mangusta 80 at €4,719, 12 guests pay €393 each. For an 8-hour Tier A boat at €2,299, 10 guests pay €230 each. Use a payment app to collect funds upfront. Don't forget to budget for extras like transfers from your hotel to Puerto Banús or a post-charter dinner at a Golden Mile restaurant.

Popular Stag Weekend Itineraries on the Water

Most stag charters start from Puerto Banús. A typical 4-hour route heads west to Cabopino or east to Cala Cortés, with stops for swimming and jet skiing. For a full day, cruise to Estepona or Sotogrande, enjoying lunch at a beach club. Your skipper can tailor the route to your group's vibe – party, relax, or both.

Tips for Booking Your Stag Yacht Charter

Book at least 2-3 weeks ahead, especially for July and August weekends. Confirm your group size (max 12 on most boats) and any dietary needs. Bring sunscreen, towels, and a Bluetooth speaker. For a stress-free experience, let us handle the logistics – just check our boat rental Marbella page for availability.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cheapest boat for a stag weekend in Marbella?

The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, both at €749 for 2 hours, are the most affordable options. They accommodate up to 11 guests and include a skipper, drinks, and snacks, making them ideal for smaller stag groups.

How many people can join a stag yacht charter in Marbella?

Most boats in our fleet, like the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge, take up to 12 passengers. Smaller boats like the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax) suit intimate groups. Always check capacity when booking.

What is included in the charter price for a stag weekend?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). No hidden costs – just show up and enjoy.

Can we bring our own food and drinks on the boat?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat already provides a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise for your stag group. Just avoid glass bottles for safety.

How long is a typical stag yacht charter in Marbella?

Most stag groups book for 4 to 8 hours. A 4-hour trip (e.g., €4,719 for Mangusta 80) covers swimming stops and cruising along the coast. For a full day, 8-hour charters from €2,299 (Tier A) offer more time for partying.

What is the best time of year for a stag weekend yacht charter?

May through October offers the best weather, with sea temperatures between 28°C and 32°C. July and August are peak season, so book early. April and November can still be pleasant but less predictable.

Are there any extra costs I should budget for?

No – the listed price includes everything except optional extras like a jet ski (free with Mangusta 80) or catering upgrades. Gratuities for the skipper are appreciated but not mandatory. Transfers to Puerto Banús are separate.

How do I book a boat for a stag weekend in Marbella?

For Tier A and B boats, you can book directly via our website. For other boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, contact us on WhatsApp for pricing and availability. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance.
